sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: GE버노바, 3분기 수주 55% 늘고 매출 12% 증가하며 가이던스 재확인

keyPoints.text: 2025년 3분기 신규 수주가 146억 달러로 유기적 기준 55% 늘며 전력·전기화 부문 장비가 성장을 이끌었다.
매출은 100억 달러로 12% 늘었고, 회사는 기존 2025년 가이던스를 재확인했다.
전기화 부문 수주잔고가 연초 이후 260억 달러 수준까지 늘며 장기 수요 강세를 보여줬다.

businessChanges.text: 데이터센터와 전력 인프라 수요를 배경으로 전 사업부에서 수주잔고가 늘며 성장 궤도가 가팔라졌다.
풍력(Wind)은 부진했지만 온쇼어 서비스가 장비 약세를 일부 상쇄했다.

financialHighlights.text: 전력(Power) 부문은 수주 78억 달러로 50%, 매출 48억 달러로 15% 늘며 성장을 주도했다.
연초 이후 수주잔고가 160억 달러 늘었고, 가스파워 장비 수주잔고·슬롯예약은 55 GW에서 62 GW로 확대됐다.
분기 순이익률은 4.5%였고, 잉여현금흐름은 흑자를 유지했으나 전년보다 감소했다.

watchItems.text: 가이던스가 상향이 아닌 재확인에 그친 만큼, 4분기 이후 수주 모멘텀이 실제 매출 성장으로 이어지는지 확인할 필요가 있다.
풍력 부문 매출이 줄고 잉여현금흐름이 전년보다 감소한 만큼, 운전자본·설비투자 부담이 현금흐름에 미치는 영향을 지켜볼 필요가 있다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: GE Vernova reports third quarter 2025 financial results and reaffirms guidance S trong 3Q'25 results with robust orders and backlog, continued margin expansion and positive free cash flow Third Quarter 2025 Highlights: • Orders of $14.6 B, + 55% organically, led by equipment at Power and Electrification • Backlog 1 growth of $ 6.6 B sequentially from equipment and services • Gas Power equipment backlog and slot reservation agreements grew from 55 to 62 GW • Revenue of $10.0 B, + 12% , + 10% organically*, with growth in both equipment and services • Net income of $0.5 B; net income margin of 4.5% • Adjusted EBITDA* of $0.8 B and adjusted EBITDA margin* of 8.1% • Cash from operating activities o f $1.0 B; free cash flow* of $ 0.7 B • $7.9 B cash balance; $2.4B in capital returned to shareholders year-to-date CAMBRIDGE, Mass., (Octo  Normalized numeric aliases: $14.6 = 14.6달러; $6.6 = 6.6달러; $10.0 = 10.0달러; $0.5 = 0.5달러; $0.8 = 0.8달러; $1.0 = 1.0달러; $0.7 = 0.7달러; $7.9 = 7.9달러; $2.4 = 2.4달러.

try leader enabling customers to accelerate the energy transition , today reported financial results for the third quarter ending September 30, 2025. “ GE Vernova delivered another productive quarter with strong financial results. Our growth trajectory is accelerating and the demand environment for our equipment and services remains strong with $16 billion in backlog growth year-to-date. Our Gas Power equipment backlog and slot reservation agreements increased from 55 to 62 gigawatts sequentially, and our Electrification equipment backlog increased $ 6.5 b illion year-to-date, to approximately $26 billion , ” said GE Vernova CEO Scott Strazik. “ We are leading from a position of strength and are focused on long-term growth and returns. This era of increased electricity investment has just started, and we have substantial opportunity ahead of us as we provide the solutions re  Normalized numeric aliases: $16 billion = 160억 달러; $26 billion = 260억 달러; $16 = 16달러; $6.5 = 6.5달러; $26 = 26달러.

p the world electrify to thrive and decarbonize . ” In the third quarter, orders o f $14.6 billion increased + 55% organically, driven by robust equipment growth at Power and Electrification, with continued services growth . R evenue of $10.0 billion was up + 12% , + 10% organically*, led by equipment at Electrification and Power, as well as services at Power, more than offsetting lower Wind equipment . Margins expanded significantly, with growth in all segments. Free cash flow* remained positive and was down year-over-year as strong adjusted EBITDA* was offset by lower positive working capital benefits and higher capex investments. Power • Orders of $7.8 billion increased + 50% organically and revenues of $4.8 billion increased + 15% , + 14% organica  Normalized numeric aliases: $14.6 billion = 146억 달러; $10.0 billion = 100억 달러; $7.8 billion = 78억 달러; $4.8 billion = 48억 달러; $14.6 = 14.6달러; $10.0 = 10.0달러; $7.8 = 7.8달러; $4.8 = 4.8달러; 120 basis points = 120포인트; 140 basis points = 140포인트.

(GW) of new gas equipment contracts including 12 GW of slot reservation agreements and 1 GW of orders. Converted 7 GW of existing slot reservation agreements to orders and shipped 4 GW of equipment; resulting in backlog growth from 29 to 33 GW and an increase in slot reservation agreements from 25 to 29 GW . Wind • Orders of $1.8 billion increased 4% organically , driven by higher Onshore Wind services, which more than offset lower Onshore Wind equipment. Revenues of $2.6 billion decreased (8)% , (9)% organically*, primarily due to the nonrecurrence of the settlement of a previously canceled Offshore Wind project in the third quarter of 202 4.
